channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: A self-regulated photothermal anti-/deicing film for all-season applications
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69494-x

A self-regulated photothermal superhydrophobic film with strong solar modulation, energy storage, and durability is reported, switching between black and white as temperature changes, enabling winter anti-/deicing and summer overheating prevention.
item.dc:title: A self-regulated photothermal anti-/deicing film for all-season applications
item.dc:creator: Jiayu Du
Wenqi Wang
Yang Fu
Xin Li
Jie Tan
Hao Li
Xu Chen
Fuqiang Chu
Qi Min
Chi Yan Tso

item.title: Dynamic guanidinium sulfate salt for selective carbon dioxide adsorption with negative pressure inflexion
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69433-w

Here authors present “negative pressure” CO2 adsorption in hydrogen-bonded salts. This behavior, driven by structural phase transitions, enables high-capacity capture (4.2 mmol/g) and regeneration for sustainable carbon management.
item.dc:title: Dynamic guanidinium sulfate salt for selective carbon dioxide adsorption with negative pressure inflexion
item.dc:creator: Li Zhao
Chengxi Zhao
Congyan Liu
Zhilin Xiang
Chiran Wang
Songlin Cui
Linjiang Chen
Bo Liu

item.title: Mechanochemical engineering of chiroptical properties in indium-based chiral metal halides by grinding
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69353-9

Nanomaterials with chiral photoluminescence are promising candidates for advanced optical technologies. Here, the authors use mechsanochemical processing to tune the emission of circularly polarized light and enhance the nonlinear optical response in indium-based halides.
item.dc:title: Mechanochemical engineering of chiroptical properties in indium-based chiral metal halides by grinding
item.dc:creator: Junhong Wu
Hao Li
Jialu Wang
Xiaorui Yang
Tianyong Zhang
Bin Li
Shuang Jiang
